message:
fix(startup): auto-recover from snapshot conflicts instead of failing with busy error

When FrankenSQLite encounters a stale WAL with pages that don't match
the main DB file (e.g. after SIGKILL), it reports a "snapshot conflict"
error. Previously this was classified as a lock/busy error and caused
`am` to refuse to start with a misleading "database is busy" message,
even though `am doctor repair --yes` would fix it instantly.

Now: snapshot conflicts are detected before the generic is_lock_error
check and routed to WAL cleanup (remove WAL+SHM sidecars) → retry →
full archive reconstruction. This matches what `doctor repair` does
manually but happens automatically on every startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
